resolution.summary: Gate was BLOCKED (below) by a runtime<->world-core API mismatch + a governance-spawn method-name bug. Both fixed by an integration-reconciliation agent (runtime.py/_apply_action rewritten to consume world-core's event-dict API via a vote-style _emit_world_result helper, passing the building_id string; api/app.py governance-spawn now calls world.enqueue_admit_agent and returns rule.id). engine/world.py was NOT touched. The 2 strict-xfail pins were promoted to real end-to-end assertions through TickLoop._execute_turn. Re-verified: 135 passed, 0 failed, 0 xfail. Orchestrator independently confirmed test_runtime_full_construction_chain_to_operational_through_loop drives the chain through the real loop and asserts operational@progress==100, credit conservation, and NO parse_failure; test_api_governance_spawn_creates_admit_rule_and_admits_on_pass passes by name. The blockers below are RESOLVED and retained for history.

contract_conformance._score_history: Was 2 at gate-time (runtime dispatch broken); restored to 5 after the integration fix — the §W7 collective-project loop now runs end-to-end through the tick loop (see resolution).
contract_conformance.notes: WORLD-CORE conforms to world-model.md §W7 and action-protocol.schema.json (v1.1.0): the Building state machine, the project pipeline, function gating, and decision caching are all correct when driven through the World.action_* API / Router directly (verified by behavior with MockProvider + FastAPI TestClient). LIFECYCLE: propose_project emits [project_proposed, structure_state_changed{none->planned}]; a full contribute emits [economy, project_funded, structure_state_changed{planned->under_construction}]; 5x build_step (step=20) emits project_built each, then at progress==100 emits [project_built, structure_state_changed{under_construction->operational}, building_operational] — operational ONLY at 100 (invariant 6). FUNCTION GATING: an operational garden/farm adds +forage_bonus at its place and an operational workshop adds +work_bonus_pct% at a work place; planned/offline/damaged/destroyed grant NO buff (invariant 6 held across all states). CACHE (EM-068): identical (profile, messages) invokes the adapter ONCE, the second is a HIT (cached=true surfaced to last_usage, input/output tokens null, latency 0, routed value preserved); different messages miss; cache_enabled=False disables; mock NEVER cached (last_usage stays None). API: POST /api/agents god -> 201 + immediate presence; governance -> 202 + proposal_id + NOT present immediately; GET /api/buildings returns the §W7 building dicts and empty-200 with no world. HOWEVER conformance is held at 2 because the RUNTIME end-to-end dispatch of all six W7 building actions is broken (see CRITICAL blocker): the contract's 'agent proposes -> contributes -> builds' loop cannot run through AgentRuntime.run_turn at all, so the §W7 actions are not conformant end-to-end.

invariants.notes: Invariant 6 (the W7 invariant) holds in world-core: a Building's function is granted ONLY while status=='operational' (asserted across operational/offline/damaged/destroyed/planned), progress in [0,100], health in [0,100], funds_committed>=0, and operational iff progress==100 (building_operational never fires before 100). Invariant 1 (credit conservation) holds for the construction sink: contribute_funds moves exactly `amount` from agent.credits to funds_committed (agent.credits + funds_committed conserved, contributor recorded once), an over-balance contribution is rejected and mutates NOTHING (no debit, no commit, no contributor), and credits never go negative. FAILURE MODES verified: a planned or under_construction building idle past abandon_after_ticks -> abandoned (one structure_state_changed; operational/offline never abandoned); arson drops health by arson_damage (operational->damaged, health 0->destroyed) and lowers co-located witness trust; repair restores health=100 and status=operational. TOOL GATING verified via _validate_world: build_step requires under_construction AND co-location; arson blocked by an active ban_arson rule; contribute affordability enforced; take_offline owner-only — every gated attempt fails WITHOUT mutating state. SPAWN: world-core enqueue_admit_agent -> agent absent -> vote passes threshold (>floor(living/2)) -> agent admitted with method=governance, drainable exactly once (idempotent, no double-spawn); a rejected vote admits nobody. Held at 4 (not 5) only because invariant 6 / invariant 1 are verified at the world-core layer, not end-to-end through the runtime (which is broken).

test_coverage.notes: Added backend/tests/test_w7.py: 36 pytest cases (34 pass + 2 strict-xfail integration pins), deterministic + offline, mirroring existing harness patterns (World/Router/Runtime wiring; MockProvider scripts; FastAPI TestClient via sys.modules['petridish.api.app'] to reach the singletons the package __init__ shadows). Coverage by brief item: (1) LIFECYCLE — 4 cases: propose/fund/build event ordering + status transitions + operational-only-at-100. (2) FUNCTION GATING — 5 cases: garden+farm forage buff, workshop work buff, all only-while-operational, no-buff across offline/damaged/destroyed. (3) CREDIT CONSERVATION — 3 cases: exact move, over-balance rejection no-mutate, never-negative. (4) FAILURE MODES — 5 cases: planned + under_construction abandon-after-window, operational-never-abandoned, arson damage->destroy + repair + witness trust, arson-on-destroyed no-op. (5) TOOL GATING — 6 cases: build_step status + co-location, arson ban, contribute affordability, take_offline owner-only, plus a runtime idle-on-gated-build_step (the validation path that does NOT reach the broken dispatch). (6) SPAWN — 4 cases: world-core governance admit on pass + reject-no-admit + drain idempotency; API god immediate (201) + governance deferred (202). (7) CACHE — 6 cases: hit-once, different-miss, miss/hit/miss, mock-never-cached, cache-disabled. (8) GET /api/buildings — 3 cases: empty-200 no buildings, building-dicts shape, no-world empty-200. Two strict-xfail cases (test_runtime_propose_project_end_to_end, test_runtime_contribute_funds_end_to_end) PIN the CRITICAL runtime-dispatch defect so the gate stays red until it is fixed; --runxfail confirms they fail for the documented reasons (ValueError unpack / Building-unhashable-as-dict-key). Full suite: 131 passed, 2 xfailed (97 prior regressions + 34 new W7 pass), 0 failed.

security.notes: Low surface, local-first, single-user, no auth/PII by design (unchanged from W6). W7-relevant: the decision cache keys on sha1(profile + json(messages, sort_keys=True)) — a stable digest over the SAME world-state-bearing messages, so a hit can only return a completion from an identical situation (it never crosses world states); the LRU is bounded (cache_max, default 512, evicts least-recently-used) so it cannot grow unbounded. contribute_funds/build_step/arson/take_offline all fail-closed on bad/typed input (non-int amount coerced to 0 -> rejected; unknown building_id -> parse_failure, never raises in world-core). The governance admit_agent path guards double-spawn with rule.applied and drains the spawn outbox idempotently. take_offline is owner-gated and arson is ban_arson-gated at validation. GET /api/buildings degrades to empty-200 (never 500) with no world. Held at 4 because the API remains unauthenticated (acceptable for local-first scope) — no NEW security defect introduced by W7.

test_results.note: Post-fix re-run. The 2 strict-xfail pins were promoted to real end-to-end loop assertions + 2 more added; all green.

blockers_resolved.description: Runtime <-> world-core W7 building-action API mismatch breaks EVERY building action end-to-end. AgentRuntime._apply_action (backend/petridish/agents/runtime.py lines 1190, 1224, 1258, 1297, 1321, 1364) dispatches the six W7 actions assuming world-core returns (ok, reason, ...) TUPLES and takes a building OBJECT, but World.action_propose_project/contribute_funds/build_step/repair/arson/take_offline (backend/petridish/engine/world.py) return EVENT DICTS ({_multi:[...]} / a single event) and take a building_id STRING. Result: any agent that chooses a building action crashes its turn (propose_project -> ValueError: not enough values to unpack (expected 3, got 2); contribute_funds -> TypeError: cannot use Building as dict key (unhashable type)). run_turn does not catch this, so _execute_turn propagates the exception and the turn dies. The entire §W7 collective-project loop is non-functional through the live tick loop. Pinned by 2 strict-xfail tests in test_w7.py. FIX: reconcile the two implementations on ONE contract (recommend the world-core event-dict API, which is correct and self-consistent) and rewrite the runtime W7 dispatch branches to consume it (mirroring how vote's _multi result is handled).

API governance-spawn never enqueues a real admit_agent proposal. backend/petridish/api/app.py line 350 calls getattr(_world, 'propose_admit_agent', None) but world-core defines enqueue_admit_agent (no propose_admit_agent). The getattr-with-None fallback silently no-ops, so POST /api/agents mode=governance returns 202 with a fabricated proposal_id but creates NO admit_agent rule — the agent can therefore NEVER be admitted via the API (no rule exists to vote on). The world-core governance mechanism itself is correct (verified by test_world_governance_admit_agent_enters_only_after_vote_passes); only the API wiring is wrong. FIX: call _world.enqueue_admit_agent(...) (or add a propose_admit_agent alias) and emit the returned rule.id as the proposal_id.

issues: petridish/api/__init__.py does `from .app import app`, so the dotted name `petridish.api.app` resolves to the FastAPI INSTANCE, not the submodule — tests that rebind _world/_loop singletons must reach the module via sys.modules['petridish.api.app']. Documented in-test; a footgun for future authors (same note as W6).

The API governance-spawn event is emitted with actor_type 'god' (app.py) even though the method is 'governance'; world-model.md §W7 specifies method:'governance' for the governance path. The payload.method is correct ('governance'), but actor_type='god' is inconsistent labeling. Cosmetic, not gating.

The runtime build-action branches (even once the tuple/dict mismatch is fixed) reference helper shapes like `transitioned` and a `new_status` return that world-core does not provide; the fix should re-derive transitions from the returned event list (the _multi kinds already encode them) rather than reintroduce a parallel tuple API.

End-to-end lifecycle coverage (brief item 1's scripted-mock-actions-through-a-run) is asserted at the world-core layer plus a runtime gating turn; the full propose->contribute->build->operational chain through TickLoop._execute_turn is intentionally NOT asserted green because it is broken — it is pinned red via strict-xfail instead. Once the CRITICAL blocker is fixed, those two xfail tests will XPASS (strict -> fail) and should be promoted to full end-to-end lifecycle assertions through the loop.

gate_decision.reason: GATE GREEN (after fix). The two blockers that originally set proceed=false (CRITICAL runtime<->world-core dispatch mismatch; HIGH governance-spawn method-name no-op) have both been fixed and re-verified — see `resolution` + `blockers_resolved`. The construction pipeline now runs end-to-end through TickLoop._execute_turn (operational@100, credits conserved, no parse_failure), governance-spawn creates a real admit_agent rule, and the full suite is 135 passed / 0 failed / 0 xfail. The previously-clean dimensions are unchanged: decision cache never crosses world states + mock-never-cached (skeptic not refuted); frontend renders buildings by status from real world_state.buildings (skeptic not refuted); world-core invariants 1 & 6 hold. No CRITICAL/HIGH outstanding; contract_conformance restored to end-to-end.
